revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:وكالة الشؤون الثقافية اليابانية]][[ast:Axencia p'Asuntos Culturales]][[ca:Agència d'Afers Culturals]][[cy:Asiantaeth Materion Diwylliannol Siapan]][[de:Bunka-chō]][[en:Agency for Cultural Affairs]][[es:Agencia para Asuntos Culturales]][[fa:دفتر امور فرهنگی]][[fr:Agence pour les Affaires culturelles]][[id:Badan Urusan Budaya]][[ja:文化庁]][[ko:일본 문화청]][[pt:Agência para Assuntos Culturais]][[ru:Агентство по делам культуры]][[tr:Kültür İşleri Ajansı]][[uk:Управління культури Японії]][[zh:文化廳]]
{{short description|Special body of the Japanese Ministry of Education|qq=}} {{Infobox government agency | name = Agency for Cultural Affairs | native_name = | native_name_a = {{lang|ja|文化庁|qq=}} | native_name_r = Bunka-chō | type = | seal = | seal_width = | seal_caption = | logo = Logo of Agency for Cultural Affairs (Bunka-chō).png | logo_width = 100 | logo_caption = | image = Agency for Cultural Affairs Kyoto Buildings 20230413.jpg | image_size = | image_caption = Office building | formed = {{Start date and age|1968|06|15|qq=}}<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1406061.html |title=文化庁創立50周年を迎えて［文化庁長官談話］ |trans-title=Commissioner's statement on the 50th anniversary of the Agency for Cultural Affairs |date=June 15, 2018 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref> | preceding1 = Cultural Bureau of the Ministry of Education | preceding2 = Committee for the Protection of Cultural Properties | dissolved = | superseding1 = | superseding2 = | jurisdiction = {{JPN|qq=}} | headquarters = 85-4 Yabunouchi-cho, Shimochoja-machi-dori, Shinmachi-nishi-iru, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyo-ku|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto-shi|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} 602-8959, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | coordinates = {{coord|35|1|14|N|135|45|22|E|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title|qq=}} | motto = | employees = 301 (2020)<ref name="Organization">{{cite web |url= https://www.mext.go.jp/en/about/organization/title01/detail01/1374476.htm |title=Organization Chart |publisher=Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology |access-date=April 2, 2021|qq=}}</ref> | budget = {{yen|108 billion|qq=}} (2018) | minister1_name = {{ill|Shunichi Tokura|en|Shunichi Tokura|qid=Q11643484|lt=Shunichi Tokura|1=Shunichi Tokura}} | minister1_pfo = Commissioner for Cultural Affairs | minister2_name = | minister2_pfo = | deputyminister1_name = | deputyminister1_pfo = | deputyminister2_name = | deputyminister2_pfo = | chief1_name = | chief1_position = | chief2_name = | chief2_position = | parent_department = {{ill|Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|en|Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|qid=Q1054379|lt=Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|1=Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology}} | parent_agency = | child1_agency = {{ill|Japan Art Academy|en|Japan Art Academy|qid=Q1632766|lt=Japan Art Academy|1=Japan Art Academy}} | child2_agency = | keydocument1 = | website = {{URL|www.bunka.go.jp|qq=}} | map = {{Location map|Japan#Japan Kyoto city |lat_deg = 35.0207 |lon_deg = 135.7562 }} | map_size = | map_caption = | footnotes = | embed = }} [[File:Monbusho2.jpg|thumb|Office building with {{ill|Kadomatsu|en|Kadomatsu|qid=Q2667233|lt=Kadomatsu|1=Kadomatsu}} in 2005]] The {{Nihongo|'''Agency for Cultural Affairs'''|文化庁|Bunka-chō|lead=yes|qq=}} is a special body of the {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}ese {{ill|Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|en|Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|qid=Q1054379|lt=Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|1=Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology}} (MEXT). It was set up in 1968 to promote Japanese arts and culture. The agency's budget for FY 2018 rose to ¥107.7 billion.<ref name="policy">{{cite report|title=Policy of Cultural Affairs in Japan Fiscal 2018 |year=2018 |pages=10, 42 |url=http://www.bunka.go.jp/english/report/annual/pdf/r1394357_01.pdf |access-date=2019-02-17|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The agency's Cultural Affairs Division disseminates information about the arts within Japan and internationally, and the [[Cultural Properties Protection Division]] protects the nation's cultural heritage. The Cultural Affairs Division is concerned with such areas as art and culture promotion, art [[:en:copyrights|copyrights]], and improvements in the {{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|qid=Q5287|lt=national language|1=Japanese language}}. It also supports both national and local arts and cultural festivals, and it funds traveling cultural events in music, theater, dance, art exhibitions, and film-making. Special prizes are offered to encourage young artists and established practitioners, and some grants are given each year to enable them to train abroad. The agency funds national museums of modern art in Kyoto and Tokyo and [[:en:The National Museum of Western Art|The National Museum of Western Art]] in Tokyo, which exhibit both Japanese and international shows. The agency also supports the {{ill|Japan Art Academy|en|Japan Art Academy|qid=Q1632766|lt=Japan Art Academy|1=Japan Art Academy}}, which honors eminent persons of arts and letters, appointing them to membership and offering ¥3.5 million in prize money. Awards are made in the presence of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}}, who personally bestows the highest accolade, the {{ill|Order of Culture|en|Order of Culture|qid=Q530124|lt=Order of Culture|1=Order of Culture}}. In 1989, for the first time two women—a writer and a costume designer—were nominated for the {{ill|Order of Culture|en|Order of Culture|qid=Q530124|lt=Order of Cultural Merit|1=Order of Culture}}, another official honor carrying the same stipend. The Cultural Properties Protection Division originally was established to oversee restorations after {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}. {{asof|April 2018|post=,|qq=}} it was responsible for 1,805 historic sites, including the ancient capitals of [[Asuka, Yamato|Asuka]], [[Heijō Palace|Heijokyo]], and {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|en|Fujiwara-kyō|qid=Q1196381|lt=Fujiwara|1=Fujiwara-kyō}}, 410 scenic places, and 1,027 national monuments, and for such indigenous fauna as {{ill|ibis|en|ibis|qid=Q193833|lt=ibis|1=Ibis}} and {{ill|stork|en|stork|qid=Q28507|lt=stork|1=Stork}}s. In addition, over 10,000 items had the lesser designation of {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Properties|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}, with fine arts and crafts accounting for the largest share, with over 10,000 so designated.<ref name="policy" /> The government protects buried properties, of which some 300,000 had been identified. During the 1980s, many important prehistoric and historic sites were investigated by the archaeological institutes that the agency funded, resulting in about 2,000 excavations in 1989. The wealth of material unearthed shed new light on the controversial period of the formation of the Japanese state. A 1975 amendment to the [[Cultural Properties Protection Act]] of 1897 enabled the Agency for Cultural Affairs to designate traditional areas and buildings in urban centers for preservation. From time to time, various endangered traditional artistic skills are added to the agency's preservation roster, such as the 1989 inclusion of a kind of ancient doll making. One of the most important roles of the Cultural Properties Protection Division is to preserve the traditional arts and crafts and performing arts through their living exemplars. Individual artists and groups, such as a dance troupe or a pottery village, are designated as ''{{ill|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q2901957|lt=mukei bunkazai|1=Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}'' (intangible cultural assets) in recognition of their skill. Major exponents of the traditional arts have been designated as ''{{ill|en:Living National Treasure (Japan)|en|en:Living National Treasure (Japan)|qid=Q1137360|lt=ningen kokuho|1=Living National Treasure (Japan)}}'' (living national treasures). About seventy persons are so honored at any one time; in 1989 the six newly designated masters were a ''[[Kyōgen|kyogen]]'' (comic) performer, a chanter of ''{{ill|Bunraku|en|Bunraku|qid=Q243170|lt=Bunraku|1=Bunraku}}'' (puppet) theater, a performer of the ''nagauta {{ill|Shamisen|en|Shamisen|qid=Q241337|lt=Shamisen|1=Shamisen}}'' (a special kind of stringed instrument), the head potter making [[:en:Imari porcelain|Nabeshima]] decorated porcelain ware, the top pictorial [[:en:lacquer-ware|lacquer-ware]] artist, and a metal-work expert. Each was provided a lifetime annual pension of ¥2 million and financial aid for training disciples. A number of institutions come under the aegis of the Agency for Cultural Affairs: the national museums of Japanese and Asian art in {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo National Museum}}, {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|en|Kyoto National Museum|qid=Q147286|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto National Museum}}, {{ill|Nara National Museum|en|Nara National Museum|qid=Q147312|lt=Nara|1=Nara National Museum}}, {{ill|National Museum of Art, Osaka|en|National Museum of Art, Osaka|qid=Q1055037|lt=Osaka|1=National Museum of Art, Osaka}} and {{ill|Kyushu National Museum|en|Kyushu National Museum|qid=Q148543|lt=Fukuoka|1=Kyushu National Museum}}, the cultural properties research institutes at Tokyo and Nara, and the national theaters. During the 1980s, the {{ill|National Noh Theatre|en|National Noh Theatre|qid=Q1138457|lt=National Noh Theatre|1=National Noh Theatre}} and the [[:en:National Bunraku Theater|National Bunraku Theater]] were constructed by the government. As of April 2021, it is led by the Commissioner for Cultural Affairs, {{ill|Shunichi Tokura|en|Shunichi Tokura|qid=Q11643484|lt=Shunichi Tokura|1=Shunichi Tokura}}. [[File:Opening ceremony of the Agency for Cultural Affairs in Kyoto 20230327.jpg|thumb|The opening ceremony of the new office was held in 2023.]] The agency is based in the {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyo Ward|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}} of {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}. Main parts of the agency moved to Kyoto in 2023, while other parts remained in Tokyo.<ref>{{Cite news|title = After Relocation to Kyoto, Cultural Affairs Agency Starts Operations |url = https://japannews.yomiuri.co.jp/politics/politics-government/20230327-99865/ |date = March 27, 2023 |newspaper = [[Yomiuri Shimbun#Other publications and ventures|The Japan News]]|qq=}}</ref> ==List of commissioners== *{{ill|Hidemi Kon|en|Hidemi Kon|qid=Q454888|lt=Hidemi Kon|1=Hidemi Kon}} (June 15, 1968 – July 1, 1972) *{{ill|Kenji Adachi|ja|安達健二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji Adachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenji Adachi|1=Kenji Adachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11451508}} (July 1, 1972 – September 12, 1975) *{{ill|Hisashi Yasujima|ja|安嶋弥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisashi Yasujima|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisashi Yasujima|1=Hisashi Yasujima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21702625}} (September 12, 1975 – September 20, 1977) *[[Tadashi Inumaru]] (犬丸直; September 20, 1977 –June 6, 1980) *[[Shinichiro Sano]] (佐野文一郎; June 6, 1980 – July 5, 1983) *{{ill|Isao Suzuki|en|Isao Suzuki|qid=Q118915|lt=Isao Suzuki|1=Isao Suzuki}} (鈴木勲; July 5, 1983 – March 31, 1985) *{{ill|Shumon Miura|en|Shumon Miura|qid=Q4297024|lt=Shumon Miura|1=Shumon Miura}} (April 1, 1985 – September 1, 1986) *{{ill|Hitoshi Ōsaki|ja|大崎仁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoshi Ōsaki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoshi Ōsaki|1=Hitoshi Ōsaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16264689}} (September 1, 1986 – June 10, 1988) *[[Hiroshi Ueki]] (植木浩; June 10, 1988 – July 1, 1990) *{{ill|Tsuneaki Kawamura|ja|川村恒明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuneaki Kawamura|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuneaki Kawamura|1=Tsuneaki Kawamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11478772}} (July 1, 1990 – July 1, 1992) *[[Hiroyuki Uchida]] (内田弘保; July 1, 1992 – July 25, 1994) *[[:en:Atsuko Tōyama|Atsuko Tōyama]] (July 25, 1994 – January 9, 1996) *Shigeru Yoshida (吉田茂; January 9, 1996 – July 1, 1997) *{{ill|Hideki Hayashida|ja|林田英樹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hideki Hayashida|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hideki Hayashida|1=Hideki Hayashida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11533219}} (July 1, 1997 – June 15, 2000) *{{ill|Masamine Sasaki|ja|佐々木正峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamine Sasaki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masamine Sasaki|1=Masamine Sasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382292}} (June 15, 2000 – January 18, 2002) *{{ill|Hayao Kawai|en|Hayao Kawai|qid=Q2025444|lt=Hayao Kawai|1=Hayao Kawai}} (January 18, 2002 – November 1, 2006) *[[Shinji Kondo]] (近藤信司; November 1, 2006 – April 1, 2007) *{{ill|Tamotsu Aoki|ja|青木保 (文化人類学者)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamotsu Aoki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamotsu Aoki|1=Tamotsu Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11661638}} (April 1, 2007 – July 14, 2009) *{{ill|Hideo Tamai|ja|玉井日出夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hideo Tamai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hideo Tamai|1=Hideo Tamai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572377}} (July 14, 2009 – July 29, 2010) *{{ill|Seiichi Kondō|ja|近藤誠一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiichi Kondō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seiichi Kondō|1=Seiichi Kondō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q116935806}} (July 30, 2010 – July 7, 2013) *{{ill|Masanori Aoyagi|ja|青柳正規|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masanori Aoyagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masanori Aoyagi|1=Masanori Aoyagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11661949}} (July 8, 2013 – April 1, 2016) *{{ill|Ryohei Miyata|ja|宮田亮平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryohei Miyata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryohei Miyata|1=Ryōhei Miyata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11455106}} (April 1, 2016 – March 31, 2021) *{{ill|Shunichi Tokura|en|Shunichi Tokura|qid=Q11643484|lt=Shunichi Tokura|1=Shunichi Tokura}} (since April 1, 2021) ==Organization== [[File:Agency for Cultural Affairs Organizational chart 20230327.png|thumb|600px|Organizational chart as of March 27, 2023.]] The agency contains the following divisions:<ref name="Organization"/><ref>{{Cite web|title = 文化庁の組織 |url = https://www.bunka.go.jp/bunkacho/soshiki/index.html |language = ja|qq=}}</ref> *Policy Division – personnel matters, budget, awards system, dissemination, research *Planning and Coordination Division – {{ill|National Diet|en|National Diet|qid=Q202384|lt=diet|1=National Diet}} matters, promotion, museums, theaters, [[:en:Independent Administrative Institutions|Independent Administrative Institutions]] *Cultural Economy and International Affairs Division – economy strategy, international cooperation *Japanese Language Division – improvement of Japanese language, education for foreigners *Copyright Division – copyrights, publishing rights, treaties *Cultural Resources Utilization Division – {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|qid=Q9259|lt=World Cultural Heritage|1=World Heritage Site}}, {{ill|Intangible cultural heritage|en|Intangible cultural heritage|qid=Q84036549|lt=Intangible cultural heritage|1=Intangible cultural heritage}}, {{ill|Japan Heritage|en|Japan Heritage|qid=Q18382798|lt=Japan Heritage|1=Japan Heritage}} *First Cultural Properties Division – [[Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural properties]] other than buildings, {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q2901957|lt=intangible cultural properties|1=Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}, conservation techniques *Second Cultural Properties Division – buildings, monuments, preservation districts *{{ill|Religion in Japan|en|Religion in Japan|qid=Q1056672|lt=Religious Affairs|1=Religion in Japan}} Division – certification, technical guidance and advice *Arts and Culture Division – liaison for organizations in Tokyo, educational standards for the arts *Culture and Creativity Division – utilization of cultural resources, support for cultural creation *Life and Culture Collaboration Division *Cultural Hub Division *Japan Art Academy ==See also== *{{ill|Culture of Japan|en|Culture of Japan|qid=Q831454|lt=Culture of Japan|1=Culture of Japan}} *{{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q858308|lt=Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}} *{{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|qid=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure (Japan)|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} *{{ill|en:Living National Treasure (Japan)|en|en:Living National Treasure (Japan)|qid=Q1137360|lt=en:Living National Treasure (Japan)|1=Living National Treasure (Japan)}} *[[:en:Japanese copyright law|Japanese copyright law]] *{{ill|Freedom of religion in Japan|en|Freedom of religion in Japan|qid=Q5500773|lt=Freedom of religion in Japan|1=Freedom of religion in Japan}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Country study|qq=}} ==External links== {{commons Category|Agency for Cultural Affairs|qq=}} *[https://www.bunka.go.jp/english/ Agency for Cultural Affairs website] **[https://www.bunka.go.jp/tokei_hakusho_shuppan/shuppanbutsu/bunkazai_pamphlet/pdf/pamphlet_en_03_ver04.pdf Cultural Properties for Future Generations]
[[en:Agency for Cultural Affairs]]
